notes: New feature gene definitions for the Dubrovnik region. Merge these entries into the 'genes' block of feature_genes.json, then run generate_feature_genes.py to produce FASTA files. The 'sequence' field will be populated by the generator.

sulfur_oxidation_like.label: Sulfur oxidation enzyme complex (Sox-like)
sulfur_oxidation_like.description: Sox pathway-like sulfur oxidation system. Enables chemolithotrophic growth using reduced sulfur compounds (H2S, thiosulfate) as the primary energy source instead of sunlight or organic carbon.
sulfur_oxidation_like.pedagogy: Reveals chemolithotrophic metabolism — organisms that extract energy from chemicals rather than sunlight or organic matter. Finding this in a cave is the 'aha' moment: life without sunlight.
sulfur_oxidation_like.notes: Key marker for cave, hydrothermal vent, and anoxic sediment environments. In Vjetrenica Cave, reduced sulfur seeps in through karst groundwater.

cave_stress_like.label: Cave/oligotrophic stress adaptation cluster
cave_stress_like.description: Gene cluster associated with survival in dark, cold, nutrient-starved subterranean environments. Encodes starvation survival proteins and low-energy-state regulators analogous to those found in cave-adapted bacteria.
cave_stress_like.pedagogy: Demonstrates evolutionary adaptation to extreme resource scarcity. Good contrast with heat_shock_like (thermal stress) — same survival imperative, completely different trigger.
cave_stress_like.notes: Not a real named gene family — it is a synthetic teaching construct representing the broader category of oligotrophic stress response genes found in cave bacteria. Keep the pedagogy honest with students.
